approach: the fold-back is a MOVE, not a duplicate, and the requester pre-authorized the Clearance it fires. mux/ becomes the sole owner of what from/ratio/env mean at the seam; the seam-convention scenarios LEAVE layout.feature. The alternative (add at mux, leave layout alone) is purely additive and needs no clearance, but it manufactures the exact defect cross-node overlap detection exists to catch: one behavior specified in two nodes makes a change to it touch both files, so two file-disjoint-looking missions are a hard collision the scenario rung cannot see.

WHICH node owns the fact is settled by the suite bar's test-vector rule rather than by preference. layout.feature's ratio outline says 'Given a split node with ratio 0.333 applied through the <backend> adapter'; swap the template domain for a direct open() carrying a ratio and the Then still holds, so the template framing is APPARATUS and the precondition is a pane:* open carrying a ratio. The scenario is a mux-layer fact wearing a layout costume.

scope is spec-only, requester-ratified: no behavior change, no --ratio/--env CLI flags, no changeset. describeRegion — the other seam member specified through layout under the same house style — is deliberately out; #10 names only the three split fields.

BLOCKER carried in, not scope creep: mux.feature has NEVER parsed as Gherkin. An Examples table declares | branch | placement | and two of its three rows carry one cell, so the whole file is rejected — its entire frozen suite is invisible to check-suite and binds to nothing. The spec gate runs check-suite fail-closed over touched suites, so this CR cannot gate without the repair. Repairing it takes the node from 0 parsed / 0 bound to 61 scenarios / 34 bound / 34 pass / 0 fail / 27 unbound. The malformed outline asserts NOTHING today, so the repair widens the contract and narrows nothing — the freeze self-clears.

a stale doc comment is corrected rather than shipped: session.ts says env is 'only meaningful for a pane:* placement' while both backends emit it at every tier and the tests pin exactly that. Load-bearing, because a layout's root pane is born by the region open and never by a split, so tier-scoping env would drop it silently. Comment-only, no behavior.

the out-of-range ratio is deliberately NOT specified. The seam renders whatever it is given (open({ratio: 1.5}) emits -l -50%); the range bar lives only in the layout schema. Freezing today's behavior into a scenario would cement a wart and block a fix, so it is a prose boundary in the README plus a follow-up.

binding is a RENAME job, not new coverage. The bridge binds a test only when its leaf title is the scenario name verbatim; the adapter tests already sit under the spec:cyber-mux/mux wrapper and already cover the behavior, but their titles are implementation-voiced and bind to nothing. One genuine gap gets a new test: ratio's suppression for tab/workspace on tmux rests on a !window guard no test exercises.

why: cold sdd-spec-judge (fresh context, independent) judged the revised mux/ node — README use cases + behavior-table row + 11 scenarios added to the frozen mux.feature — and the paired layout/ narrowing, backward against {oracle,builder,architect}: all PASS, ALIGNED true, zero failing scenarios, no blocker, no open markers. Mechanical check-suite + check-spec-state green. It re-derived the edit classification structurally rather than trusting the brief (gherkin-cli diff --base HEAD mux.feature: 11 added / 0 modified / 0 removed, addOnly true, so the freeze self-clears; --base origin/main layout.feature: 0 added / 0 modified / 3 removed, exactly the scenarios claimed to move). It verified EVERY backend claim against the adapter source rather than this CR's prose — herdr's pane id is positional and no --from flag exists, toTmuxSize(0.333) really is 67%, the !window guards, env emitted at every tier on both backends, herdr's worktree create/open accepting env in their type and never referencing it, canSizeSplits true on both. It ran the miss test over all 11 new scenarios and found none inert, and ran the absorption check in the direction that threatened this CR (scenarios authored AFTER reading the implementation): no apparatus leaked into a Given, and it established that the flag literals are not a new style but the same literals layout.feature already carried. It checked pairwise consistency across all 72 scenarios and found no contradiction. Two findings worth recording. FIRST, it independently confirmed the load-bearing blocker rather than taking it on trust: it stashed the working tree and re-ran the differ against the committed baseline, reproducing EPARSE — origin/main's mux.feature genuinely does not parse, so the entire frozen suite was invisible and bound to nothing, and the repair (242ce5d) widens a scenario that asserted nothing. SECOND, it checked the lifecycle carve-out this CR could have abused: the pure-move/rename exemption (zero content delta preserves freeze) does NOT apply here because the moved scenarios were reworded, and it confirmed this CR does not claim it — the narrowing is named as a Clearance that the requester pre-authorized at intake. Three non-blocking observations carried forward: the README's no-validation boundary has no scenario by design (D4, to avoid freezing a wart, filed as a follow-up); the ratio-is-a-split-concept scenario is tmux-only because herdr's size variable is lexically scoped inside the pane-split branch and cannot leak; and canSizeSplits' silence-means-cannot reading is documented at mux while the consuming degrade lives in layout, which does not distinguish undefined from explicit false. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by user) — ratify or kick back.

TWO cold sdd-impl-judge rounds, each a fresh context re-deriving every frozen scenario's oracle from the adapter source rather than from this CR's prose. Round 1 returned CHANGE on two real defects, both found by running things rather than reading them, and the first would have shipped. R1 BLOCKER: the src/session.ts doc-comment correction was NEVER COMMITTED — the spec commit's own message claims it, but the staging line added only the spec directory, so the file stayed uncommitted working-tree drift across all three commits. At HEAD the shipped interface doc still said env is 'only meaningful for a pane:* placement', directly contradicting the scenario this CR had just frozen. No test could catch it (the change is a comment) and the message asserted it was done; the judge caught it only by running git show --name-only per commit instead of trusting the message. Committed as its own unit (f5dcd17). R1 second finding: the herdr test bound to 'env with no launch opens a blank shell carrying the env' under-verified its own Then — it ruled out `pane run` but not `send-text`/`send-keys`, where the tmux twin's single send-keys check is complete because tmux funnels all typing through one verb. Proven by mutation, not asserted: a stray bare-Enter submit on env-with-no-launch left all 73 herdr tests green. A rename-induced over-claim — the retitled test promised more than its body checked. Widened to all three verbs; the mutation now fails it. R1 also found the sibling of a defect this mission had already self-caught: the !window guard on tmux's `from` is redundant exactly as the one on `size` is, since the window branch spreads neither — documented in D7, guards deliberately left in place because removing them is a production change outside a spec-only CR. Round 2 independently re-ran all three mutations by hand rather than confirming from the report — reproducing the stray-submit catch, the single-guard-removal staying green, and the compound guard-plus-window-spread failing — and went past round 1 to check every renamed test for the same over-claim shape, finding none, plus that all 6 Examples rows of the env-tier scenario are individually bound and that scenario 10 verifies createInWorkspace AND openInWorkspace separately. IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true, all 11 frozen scenarios pass, zero failing, no blocker, no absorption findings. Bridge: 45/72 bound, 45 pass, 0 fail, 27 unbound — every one of this CR's 11 scenarios binds and passes, and the 27 unbound are the untouched pre-existing baseline, so this CR adds no unbound debt. The move also RETIRED debt rather than shifting it: the three scenarios that left layout were themselves among its unbound, so layout drops from 41 unbound to 38 and the facts are now verified at mux instead of merely stated at layout. pnpm verify green, 419 tests. Scope confirmed spec-only across all five commits — only session.ts's comment touches production code, and the judge checked no behavior was smuggled into it. Rebased onto the target tip before the gate, so the judges saw the tree that lands. One round-2 observation acted on rather than deferred: a pre-existing blank line severed the mux README's behavior table, so this CR's new row rendered under no header, and the stale duplicate placement row below it was a strict subset of the row above; both removed. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by user) — ratify or kick back.

title: The pane seam renders an out-of-range ratio without complaint
note: open({ratio: 1.5}) emits a negative tmux length and ratio: 0 emits a full-region one; nothing at the seam rejects either. The 0 < ratio < 1 bar exists only in the layout schema, so a caller reaching the adapter directly gets no check. Deliberately left unspecified by CR 10 rather than frozen: writing today's behavior into a scenario would cement a wart and block the fix. The open question is whether the seam should validate at all or whether the range is properly the caller's, which is a real design call and not a bug report. Recorded as a prose boundary on the pane node meanwhile.

title: herdr's worktree verbs drop env silently
note: herdr's worktree create/open take no env parameter, so env passed to them is dropped rather than refused. CR 10 specified the behavior as it stands because tests already pin it, but specifying it is not endorsing it: a caller asking for env and silently not getting it is the same shape of quiet failure the split-target work existed to remove. Worth deciding whether it should be loud.

title: tmux's !window guards on from and size are dead defense
note: Neither value is spread into the new-window branch, so removing either guard changes no emitted argv and fails no test. Found by mutation during CR 10 and documented there; the guards were kept because they cost nothing, state the intent locally, and removing them is a production change a spec-only CR should not make. The question for a later CR is whether to drop them as dead code or keep them as intent — either is defensible, and today the codebase does not say which it means.

title: 27 of the mux node's 61 pre-existing scenarios are UNBOUND
note: The verify-scenarios bridge reports 45/72 bound for cyber-mux/mux after CR 10, whose own 11 all bind. The 27 unbound predate it and were invisible until CR 10 repaired the Examples table that stopped the file parsing at all — before that the node reported 0 scenarios and the whole suite was unbound without anyone seeing it. The cause is per-test rather than structural: the adapter and CLI test files already sit under the spec:cyber-mux/mux wrapper, but their leaf titles are implementation-voiced, and the bridge keys on the title being the scenario name verbatim. Renaming them would bind most of the 27. Not absorbed into CR 10 because it is orthogonal to split options and touches tests for behavior that CR did not change. Consequence while it stands: the impl-judge hand-judges those 27 every round, which is the token burn the bridge exists to remove. Sibling of the same finding filed for the layout node's unbound set.

title: toTmuxSize's self-inverse and rounding boundary are unpinned
note: Only ratio 0.333 -> 67% is asserted. toTmuxSize(0.5) -> 50% is the case where the inversion is invisible because the value is its own complement — precisely the input that would pass whether or not the conversion is applied — and no test covers it, nor any rounding boundary. Cheap to add and it is the one input that cannot distinguish a correct implementation from a missing inversion.
